name: Machine Neck Extension (4-Way Neck Machine)
resistance_profile: Guided lever loads cervical extension smoothly; the padded head contact keeps resistance fairly even through the range, peaking mid-range.
cues: Move only the head and keep the torso locked
Smooth tempo through a pain-free range
Stop at neutral and do not hyperextend
execution_steps: Set the seat height so the padded lever contacts the back of your head.
Sit tall, brace your torso, and hold the handles.
Start with the chin drawn slightly toward the chest for a gentle stretch.
Push the pad back by extending your neck to a neutral position.
Pause, then return slowly to the start under control.
good_when: You want stable, easy-to-load neck extension without balancing a plate or harness
You are a beginner or rehab client who benefits from guided resistance
bad_when: The machine's pad or seat cannot fit your head and neck comfortably
common_errors: Pushing with the torso instead of the neck
Using too much load and cutting the range short
progressions: Add reps or a slower tempo
